apoie.missionSub: Fauna is a non-profit platform built by people who believe the biodiversity crisis is the greatest challenge of our generation — and that well-made technology can be part of the solution.
apoie.finP1: When you donate $20 to a conservation project on Fauna, $20 reaches the organization responsible for that project. There is no platform fee, no intermediary taking a cut.
apoie.finP2: To keep the platform running — servers, development, organization verification, support — we depend on supporters who believe in the mission and contribute directly to Fauna.

cta.title: Still have questions?
cta.subtitle: Our team is here to help.

sections.label: General questions
sections.description: What Fauna is, how it works, languages, and financial transparency.

items.q: What is Fauna?
items.a: Fauna is a global hub for animal conservation. We connect organizations working to protect wildlife with donors and supporters — both individual and institutional — from around the world, offering a platform for content, project discovery, and direct donations.

items.q: Is Fauna an NGO?
items.a: Fauna is a digital platform, not an NGO. We do not run conservation projects directly — we are the infrastructure that connects those doing fieldwork with those who want to support them.

items.q: Does Fauna take a cut of donations?
items.a: No. Donations go directly from the donor's account to the Stripe account of the chosen organization. Fauna does not manage the money and it never passes through our account. Any processing fees are charged by Stripe.

items.q: How does Fauna sustain itself financially?
items.a: Through voluntary contributions directly to the platform and through sales from our shop. Our existence does not depend on taking a commission from donations.

items.q: In which languages is the platform available?
items.a: Fauna is available in Portuguese, English, and Spanish. We accept organizations and donors from any country in the world.

items.q: Is Fauna responsible for how organizations use donations?
items.a: Fauna carries out a verification process for every registered organization, but does not audit the use of funds after donations are made. Donations go directly from the donor's account to the organization's Stripe account, without Fauna acting as intermediary. Responsibility for the use of funds rests entirely with the receiving organization. If you identify irregularities in any project, please contact us at contatofauna@proton.me.

sections.label: For organizations
sections.description: Registration, documents, projects, receiving donations, and Stripe accounts.

items.q: Can any organization register?
items.a: Yes, as long as it works on wildlife conservation projects. Every organization goes through an evaluation process before being published on the platform and receives a verification badge.

items.q: What's the advantage of registering a project on Fauna?
items.a: Fauna offers global visibility for animal conservation projects, connecting organizations with a network of engaged donors and supporters. Registration is free, donations go directly to the organization without financial intermediation, and each project gets a dedicated page with visual resources and updates.

items.q: If an organization already fundraises on its own, can it still list projects on Fauna?
items.a: Yes. Fauna is not exclusive with respect to other fundraising channels. The platform works as an additional visibility and donation channel, complementing strategies the organization already uses.

items.q: Does Fauna charge any fee for registration?
items.a: No. Registration is completely free. Fauna does not charge membership, monthly fees, or commissions on donations. We are funded through voluntary contributions directly to the platform and through our product shop — which means 100% of the amount donated to a project goes directly to the responsible organization.

items.q: Can international organizations register?
items.a: Yes. Fauna is a global platform and accepts organizations from any country. For registration, valid legal documentation from the organization's home country is required. The verification process is the same regardless of location.

items.q: In which languages can I publish my project?
items.a: Projects may be published in Portuguese and/or English. Since Fauna is a global platform, we recommend publishing in both languages to broaden reach and increase the chances of being found by donors in different countries.

items.q: Does Fauna promote my organization on social media?
items.a: Yes, provided your organization has Fauna's verification badge and active projects on the platform. To request promotion, send an email to contatofauna@proton.me with your organization's information and any marketing materials you'd like to share. Our team will review and get back to you.

items.q: What documents are required for registration?
items.a: To ensure donor safety, Fauna requires all organizations to hold some form of valid legal registration in their home country — such as CNPJ (Brazilian business tax ID), a registered association, foundation, or international equivalent — along with evidence of active projects. We may request activity reports or materials demonstrating the work performed. Fully informal projects without any legal formalization are not accepted on the platform at this time. If your initiative is in the process of formalization, contact us at contatofauna@proton.me so we can review your case.

items.q: How long does the review take?
items.a: On average 5 to 10 business days after complete documentation is submitted.

items.q: Can I register more than one project?
items.a: Yes. There is no limit on the number of projects per organization. We recommend that each project have complete and up-to-date information.

items.q: How do I receive donations?
items.a: Directly in your Stripe account. Fauna does not intermediate the payment — the connection is made directly between the donor and your account.

items.q: Does my organization need a Stripe account to receive donations?
items.a: Yes. Since donations are processed directly by Stripe, the organization needs an active and valid Stripe account in its country to receive contributions. Stripe is available in more than 40 countries. If your organization is in a country where Stripe does not yet operate, contact us at contatofauna@proton.me so we can evaluate alternatives.

items.q: Can I edit or close a project after publishing it?
items.a: Yes. Projects can be edited or closed at any time from the organization dashboard. When you close a project, it stops appearing publicly on the platform and no longer receives new contributions. All edits and project history are saved in the dashboard.

items.q: Can Fauna suspend my organization?
items.a: Yes. We reserve the right to suspend organizations that do not meet our verification criteria, that provide false information, or that violate our Terms and Conditions.

sections.label: For donors
sections.description: Security, payment methods, receipts, tax deductions, and recurring donations.

items.q: Is my donation safe?
items.a: Yes. All payments are processed by Stripe, one of the most secure payment platforms in the world. Fauna does not store donors' financial data.

items.q: Which payment methods can I use to donate?
items.a: Donations are processed by Stripe and accept credit cards, debit cards, and other methods available in your region. PIX is not yet available as a payment method, but we are working to add it soon.

items.q: What's the minimum donation amount?
items.a: Fauna has no minimum amount. Stripe may apply a minimum processing amount, which can vary. Any contribution, however small, is welcome and goes directly to the chosen project.

items.q: Can I choose the specific project I want to donate to?
items.a: Yes. You browse available projects and choose exactly where your contribution goes.

items.q: Will I receive a donation receipt?
items.a: Stripe automatically sends a payment receipt. For tax receipts or specific documentation, contact the beneficiary organization directly.

items.q: Can I cancel a recurring donation?
items.a: Yes. Recurring donations can be cancelled at any time directly in your platform account or via Stripe.

items.q: How do I know a project is legitimate?
items.a: All organizations go through verification before being published. We require legal documentation and evidence of active projects.

items.q: Is my donation tax-deductible?
items.a: Fauna is a connection platform and does not issue tax receipts. Donations made through Fauna are not tax-deductible via the platform. If you need documentation for tax purposes, contact the beneficiary organization directly — some organizations have legal qualification to issue deductible receipts in their home country.

items.q: I am an organization or foundation and would like to financially support a project on Fauna. Can I donate through the site?
items.a: Institutional donations — made by organizations, foundations, or companies — are not processed directly through the site. In this case, contact us at contatofauna@proton.me indicating which project you would like to support and your organization's details. We will connect both parties directly.

privacy.disclaimer: This privacy policy was originally drafted under Brazilian LGPD. Pending legal review for your jurisdiction (GDPR, CCPA, etc.).

sections.heading: 1. Who we are
sections.body: Fauna is a crowdfunding platform for biodiversity conservation projects. Our commitment is to protect the personal data of those who entrust us with information to support environmental causes.

sections.heading: 2. Data we collect
sections.body: We collect only what is necessary to operate the platform:
list: **Registration data:** full name and email address.
**Donation data:** amount, currency, and optional message. Payment data is processed directly by Stripe and is not stored on our servers.
**Browsing data:** access logs, IP address, and technical cookies essential to the functioning of the site.

sections.heading: 3. How we use your data
list: Authenticate your account and protect access.
Send updates about projects you support (you may unsubscribe at any time).
Comply with legal and tax obligations.

sections.heading: 4. Legal basis (LGPD)
sections.body: We process your data based on: (a) contract performance, when you make a donation; (b) consent, for email communications; and (c) compliance with legal obligations.

sections.heading: 5. Data sharing
sections.body: We do not sell or rent your data. We share only with:
list: **Supabase** — secure data storage on the database infrastructure.
sections.footer: Both providers operate in compliance with GDPR and LGPD.

sections.heading: 6. Data retention
sections.body: We keep your data while your account is active or for the period required by law (generally 5 years for financial records). You may request deletion at any time.

sections.heading: 7. Your rights
sections.body: Under LGPD you have the right to: access, correction, deletion, portability, and revocation of consent. Send your request to privacidade@fauna.org.

sections.heading: 8. Cookies
sections.body: We use essential technical cookies and preference cookies (language). See our Cookie Policy for more details.

sections.heading: 9. Security
sections.body: We use in-transit (TLS) and at-rest encryption, role-based access control, and magic-link authentication — without password storage.

cookies.disclaimer: This cookie policy was originally drafted under Brazilian LGPD. Pending legal review for your jurisdiction (GDPR ePrivacy, CCPA, etc.).
cookies.whatBody: Cookies are small text files stored in your browser when you visit a site. They allow the site to remember your preferences and keep you authenticated between visits.
cookies.thirdPartyBody: Stripe may set cookies during the payment process for anti-fraud security purposes. These cookies are controlled by Stripe and follow Stripe's privacy policy.
dontUseList: Tracking or behavior analysis cookies (Google Analytics, Meta Pixel, etc.)
Social media cookies (except if you click external links)
cookies.controlBody: You can configure your browser to block or delete cookies. Note that blocking essential cookies (sb-*) will prevent login on the platform.
cookies.doubtsBody: Contact us via the contact form or at privacidade@fauna.org.

hero.subtitle: Whether it's about donations, partnerships with organizations, press, or anything else — we're here.

about.missionP1: Fauna is a non-profit digital platform built by people who believe the biodiversity crisis is the biggest challenge of our generation — and that well-designed technology can be part of the solution.
about.missionP2: We connect conservation organizations with supporters around the world, with full transparency and zero fees on donations.

values.title: Full transparency
values.desc: 100% of donations go directly to the projects. Fauna does not keep a cent of conservation supporters' contributions.

values.title: Verified impact
values.desc: Every organization on the platform is manually verified before publishing projects. Only legitimate causes reach you.

values.title: Global access
values.desc: A project in the Pantanal can receive support from someone in Norway. We remove the bureaucratic and currency barriers that isolate conservation.

values.title: Technology as a tool
values.desc: We build digital infrastructure that conservation organizations — usually small and without IT resources — could not build on their own.

team.storyP1: Fauna was born from the realization that amazing conservation organizations were invisible to the world — not for lack of work or purpose, but for lack of digital infrastructure.
team.storyP2: Field researchers without access to fundraising tools. Urgent projects without visibility. Donors with no way to find legitimate causes. Fauna emerged to solve exactly that.
team.storyP3: We are a small team with a big belief: that well-built technology can multiply the impact of those already making a difference.

members.name: Victoria

members.bio: Designers and engineers who believe the best technology serves causes that truly matter.

success.title: Order confirmed
success.description: You'll receive an email with the order details and tracking as soon as shipping is done.
success.impact: 15% of the amount went to conservation projects. Thank you!

sections.heading: Deadline for exchange or return
sections.body:
You have up to **7 calendar days** after receiving your order to request an exchange or return, under the Brazilian Consumer Protection Code (Art. 49).
For manufacturing defects, the deadline is **30 days** from receipt.

sections.heading: Exchange conditions
list: Product unused, unwashed, and undamaged by the customer

sections.heading: How to request
orderedList: Contact us at loja@fauna.org with the order number
Describe the reason for exchange and, if possible, send photos of the product
Wait for our team to confirm (up to 2 business days)
Ship the product — we provide the return label for defect-related returns

sections.heading: Refund
sections.body: After we receive and inspect the product, the refund is processed within **5 business days** to the original card or via Pix. For exchanges for a different size or product, the new shipment goes out within **3 business days**.

sections.heading: Return shipping
sections.body: Return due to **defect or our error**: shipping on us. Return due to **change of mind**: shipping on the customer.

sections.heading: Questions
sections.body: Reach out through the contact form or at loja@fauna.org.
